settings_hierarchy: Enterprise — organization-wide policies (managed by admins, cannot be overridden)
Project — .claude/settings.json checked into the repo (this file)
_documentation.why_project_tier: This settings file lives at the Project tier because governance hooks and permission rules should be shared across all developers working on this repo. They are version-controlled, peer-reviewed, and consistent for every team member. Enterprise tier would be appropriate for org-wide policies; User/Local tiers are for personal preferences that don't affect safety.

PreToolUse.description: Block dangerous bash commands (rm -rf, DROP TABLE, git push --force, etc.)
Scan file edits/writes for leaked API keys, passwords, tokens, and private keys
Enforce file scope — only allow edits to approved paths, block frozen weeks and sensitive files

PostToolUse.description: Log every tool action to audit.jsonl with timestamp, user, branch, tool, input, result

UserPromptSubmit.description: Log all user prompts to prompts.jsonl for compliance and session review

Stop.description: Generate session summary (files modified, blocks triggered, tokens used, estimated cost)
